hooks.command: cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); printf '%s' "$cmd" | grep -qiE 'gh pr (create|ready)' || exit 0; [ -x .keiko-scripts/audit-gate.sh ] || exit 0; .keiko-scripts/audit-gate.sh >&2 || { echo '[audit-gate] PR blocked — run keiko-issue-audit first (proof of audit required before PR-ready).' >&2; exit 2; }; exit 0

cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); printf '%s' "$cmd" | grep -qiE 'gh pr (create|ready)' || exit 0; [ -x .keiko-scripts/verify-gate.sh ] || exit 0; .keiko-scripts/verify-gate.sh >&2 || { echo '[verify-gate] PR blocked — verify.sh must pass green (run verify-receipt.sh, loop until green) before PR.' >&2; exit 2; }; exit 0

cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); printf '%s' "$cmd" | grep -qiE 'gh pr merge' || exit 0; [ -x .keiko-scripts/epic-merge-gate.sh ] || exit 0; .keiko-scripts/epic-merge-gate.sh "$cmd" >&2 || exit 2; exit 0

cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); printf '%s' "$cmd" | grep -qiE 'gh pr ready' || exit 0; [ -x .keiko-scripts/ready-gate.sh ] || exit 0; .keiko-scripts/ready-gate.sh "$cmd" >&2 || { echo '[ready-gate] ready blocked — user-facing PR needs its manual-test-plan comment first.' >&2; exit 2; }; exit 0

cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); printf '%s' "$cmd" | grep -qE 'git +push' || exit 0; [ -x .keiko-scripts/push-gate.sh ] || exit 0; .keiko-scripts/push-gate.sh >&2 || { echo '[push-gate] repush to ->dev PR blocked — re-run verify + audit (+ui-verify) at HEAD first.' >&2; exit 2; }; exit 0

fp=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty'); printf '%s' "$fp" | grep -q 'agent-memory' && { echo '[memory-path] BLOCKED: agent memory lives in .agents/memory/<role>/, not the legacy .claude/agent-memory/. Write the canonical path instead.' >&2; exit 2; }; exit 0
echo 'Context rehydrated. CLAUDE.md rules: coordinator role, spec-first, wait-for-approval, conventional commits, target branch dev, never edit code yourself, delegate to teammates.'
LOG=~/.claude/agent-activity.log; [ -f "$LOG" ] && [ "$(wc -l < "$LOG" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)" -gt 2000 ] && tail -1000 "$LOG" > "$LOG.tmp" && mv "$LOG.tmp" "$LOG" || true
echo '[context] CLAUDE.md loaded - coordinator role active'
echo '[quality-reminder] Before acting: (1) read memory, (2) scope the task, (3) check existing patterns, (4) consider edge cases'
printf '[%s] Subagent spawned\n' "$(date +%H:%M:%S)" >> ~/.claude/agent-activity.log
printf '[%s] Subagent finished\n' "$(date +%H:%M:%S)" >> ~/.claude/agent-activity.log

input=$(cat); agent=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.agent_type // empty'); [ -z "$agent" ] && exit 0; mem=".agents/memory/$agent/MEMORY.md"; [ ! -f "$mem" ] && exit 0; size=$(wc -c < "$mem" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); if [ "$size" -lt 800 ]; then echo "[memory-reminder] $agent MEMORY.md is only ${size}B — append high-signal findings from this session before going idle. Aim for codebase-specific gotchas, patterns, or gotcha-workarounds. See .agents/memory/README.md" >&2; fi; exit 0
echo 'Task created - remember: verify before completing'
echo 'Task completed - verify evidence before proceeding to next wave'
echo 'Teammate idle - consider spawning next wave or cleanup'

input=$(cat); agent=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.agent_type // empty'); [ -z "$agent" ] && exit 0; mem=".agents/memory/$agent/MEMORY.md"; [ ! -f "$mem" ] && exit 0; size=$(wc -c < "$mem" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); if [ "$size" -lt 800 ]; then echo "[memory-reminder] teammate $agent MEMORY.md is only ${size}B — capture any reusable findings before idle" >&2; fi; exit 0
osascript -e 'display notification "Claude Code needs your attention" with title "Claude Code - Keiko"' 2>/dev/null || true
echo 'Context compaction starting - critical state should be saved to memory'
echo 'Compaction complete. Reminder: you are the coordinator, never edit code yourself - delegate to teammates.'

jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty' | while read f; do case "$f" in *.ts|*.tsx|*.js|*.jsx|*.mjs|*.cjs|*.json|*.css|*.scss|*.md) npx --no-install prettier --write "$f" 2>/dev/null || true ;; esac; done
echo '[quality] tool failure - investigate root cause, never retry blindly' >&2

hooks.prompt: You are the quality gate. Review the work completed this session against the original task. LOOP CAP: if `stop_hook_active` is true in your input, you have ALREADY blocked once this session — respond {"ok": true} now and let the session end (do not block a second time; escalate to the human instead of looping). First determine whether this session produced a code diff (Edit/Write tool calls that modified source files). If NO code diff was produced (analysis-only, research, planning, or documentation session), treat criteria (4), (6), and (7) as "n/a" — they do not gate analysis tasks and must not be reported as unmet. Check: (1) All acceptance criteria met with evidence, (2) No uncovered edge cases (null, empty, boundary, concurrent, error paths), (3) Type-safety is strict (no any), (4) Tests cover changed behavior [n/a without code diff], (5) No refactoring debt introduced, (6) Security implications addressed [n/a without code diff], (7) Performance not regressed [n/a without code diff]. Block ONLY for a concrete, material gap — never for style or polish. Respond with {"ok": true} unless an APPLICABLE criterion has a real defect. Otherwise {"ok": false, "reason": "concrete gap to address"} so work continues.
